Section.Label: Chapter_Orientations_of_matroids_Section_Find_orientations_of_a_given_geometric_lattice
Section.Heading: Find orientations of a given geometric lattice

ManSection.Description:
Computes all oriented matroids (up to some isomorphisms) which have L as underlying matroid structe or geometric lattice.
Depending on this size of L this might take a considerable amount of time and there may be many orientations depending on the sparsety of relations in L. The algorithm reformulates the problem of determining orientations to an SAT-instance and then uses an SAT-solver (currently PicoSAT as a standard) to solve.
gap> A:=AGpql(2,2,3); L:=IntersectionLattice(A); Bs:=LBases(L);; Length(Bs); <HyperplaneArrangement: 6 hyperplanes in 3-space> <Geometric lattice: 6 atoms, rank 3> 16 gap> OMs:=FindOrientations(L); [ <OrientedMatroid: 6 elements, rank 3>, <OrientedMatroid: 6 elements, rank 3>, <OrientedMatroid: 6 elements, rank 3>, <OrientedMatroid: 6 elements, rank 3> ]
